races.office_note: One of Wisconsin's eight seats in the U.S. House of Representatives, which writes federal law and controls federal spending. The 7th District covers much of northern and central Wisconsin, including the Wausau area. Two-year term.
races.context: Rep. Tom Tiffany is leaving this seat to run for governor, giving northern and central Wisconsin its first open congressional race since 2020. Five Republicans and three Democrats are on the primary ballot, including a Wausau candidate on the Republican side.

coverage.headline: 7th Congressional District candidates to participate in July debates
Elections Commission grants ballot access to 7th CD candidates
7th District Republican challenges rivals' nominating papers
Duffy son-in-law faces criticism for role in road trip reality show
Paul Wassgren drops out of race for 7th Congressional District
Half the candidates for Wisconsin House seat recently lived outside district or state
Meet the candidates in Wisconsin's 7th Congressional District Democratic primary

positions.topic: Enforce the Laws We Already Have
positions.statement: This country has plenty of laws. What we need more of is elected officials with the spine to enforce the laws we already have and use every tool at their disposal to stop this administration in its tracks.
Donald Trump. Kristi Noem (you're fired!). Pam Bondi (you're fired!). RFK Jr. Pete Hegseth. Marco Rubio.

ICE cannot be fixed. The culture is broken, the tactics are lawless, and the institutional contempt for due process and human dignity runs too deep to patch up with new leadership and a fresh coat of paint.

candidates.name: Fred Clark
candidates.bio: Former director of the conservation group Wisconsin's Green Fire and a former Department of Natural Resources forester. Represented the Baraboo area in the state Assembly.

positions.statement: In Northern Wisconsin, many working families just can't keep up. In the last 50 years the costs of housing, healthcare, childcare, transportation, and college or post-high school education have all risen dramatically faster than wages.

There is no reason the wealthiest country in the world should live with the most expensive health care in the world, that leaves millions without coverage, while insurance companies reap record profits.

positions.topic: Supporting Women's Rights and Women's Health
positions.statement: As a candidate and as a former legislator I have firmly supported women's freedom to make choices about reproductive care with their doctors, and I have consistently supported funding and access to a full range of reproductive health care for women of all ages including contraception.

positions.topic: Murray Speaks Out Against Costs of War
positions.statement: Americans should not be burdended with the cost of President Trump's war against Iran.
"This war was unprovoked, unwarranted, and unconstitutional," said Ginger Murray, responding to the President's decision to begin military action in Iran.

positions.topic: Ginger Murray Calls For An End To Dark Money In Politics
positions.statement: Dark money is drowning out the voices of everyday Americans.

candidates.name: Michael Alfonso
candidates.bio: Son-in-law of U.S. Transportation Secretary and former 7th District Rep. Sean Duffy.

positions.statement: Young Americans shouldn't be priced out of homeownership. Michael Alfonso will rein in spending, cut red tape, and make it possible for young families to own homes again and build secure, hopeful futures.

Strong farms mean strong families, and strong families mean a strong America. Michael Alfonso will cut burdensome restrictions, support local agriculture, and make healthy food affordable so we can fight chronic illness and prevent childhood obesity.

Parents — not bureaucrats — should decide what's best for their children. Michael Alfonso will support school choice, boost homeschooling protections, and end Marxist race and gender indoctrination in classrooms.

candidates.name: Jessi Ebben
candidates.bio: Born in Stevens Point. Ran in the 2020 Republican primary in the 3rd Congressional District.

positions.statement: Jessi is proud to have knocked thousands of doors for President Donald J. Trump and other conservative candidates. Like President Trump, she believes the American Dream is slipping out of reach because of previously enacted bad policy by weak leaders.

A nation without secure borders cannot remain free or safe. Jessi will support finishing the wall, stopping the flow of illegal drugs like fentanyl into our communities, and giving law enforcement the tools they need to secure the border.
Jessi is a card-carrying member of the National Rifle Association. She is also a member of Gun Owners of America and Hunter Nation.

candidates.name: Kevin Hermening
candidates.bio: Former Marathon County Republican Party chair and Mosinee School Board member.

positions.statement: Large-scale AI and data center development is increasingly targeting rural communities, often with promises of economic growth that do not always reflect the full long-term costs.
I grew up on a northern Wisconsin dairy farm, and today almost all of the farms that once operated in my community no longer exist.
I have spent my career working in and alongside rural communities, and I have put that commitment into action.

candidates.name: Paul Wassgren

positions.statement: Paul has championed Opportunity Zones and worked closely with President Trump's team to expand economic growth across the country during his first term.
Our judicial system has been weaponized by bad actors looking to settle scores and make money.
Social problems like addiction, homelessness, and violent crime have become far too common in our country and even in the Seventh Congressional District.

candidates.status_note: Suspended his campaign on April 21, 2026, according to Wisconsin Public Radio. His name remains on the primary ballot.

races.office_note: The state's chief executive: signs or vetoes bills passed by the Legislature, proposes the state budget, appoints agency heads, and fills vacancies in state offices and courts. Four-year term.
races.context: Gov. Tony Evers is not seeking a third term, making this Wisconsin's first open race for governor since 2010. Seven Democrats and two Republicans are on the primary ballot.

coverage.headline: Hughes suspends campaign for Wisconsin governor, endorses Rodriguez
Sara Rodriguez leads field for governor in Wisconsin Dems' straw poll
Where the race for governor stands as August primary approaches
Democrats vying for Wisconsin governor sound off on data centers, climate accountability during forum
Wisconsin's wide-open 2026 Democratic primary for governor

candidates.name: Mandela Barnes
candidates.bio: Lieutenant governor from 2019 to 2023 and the 2022 Democratic nominee for U.S. Senate. Former state representative.

positions.topic: Tax The Rich To Lower Costs For Wisconsin Families
positions.statement: Corporate greed is hollowing out Wisconsin—killing family farms, shipping jobs overseas, and leaving working families to foot the bill while out-of-state corporations pay nothing in taxes.
Wisconsin's utility monopolies rake in some of the highest profit margins in the entire nation while our bills skyrocket. That's not a coincidence.

positions.topic: Expand BadgerCare, Create Public Option and Forgive Medical Debt
positions.statement: As Governor, I will expand BadgerCare on Day One and pass a public option.

candidates.name: Joel Brennan
candidates.occupation: Former state Department of Administration secretary
candidates.bio: Served in Gov. Evers' cabinet as administration secretary. Former Discovery World president and Greater Milwaukee Committee president.

positions.statement: Joel grew up one of 11 kids. His family had to make every dollar count.

positions.topic: PROTECTING OUR WISCONSIN WAY OF LIFE
positions.statement: Wisconsin is a place where we believe in working hard, playing by the rules, and looking out for each other. Our government should do the same.

positions.topic: BUILDING A GOVERNMENT THAT WORKS FOR YOU
positions.statement: Joel spent 25 years building things that work — a science museum, a state budget, public-private partnerships, a COVID relief program. He's not interested in chaos or credit.

candidates.name: David Crowley
candidates.bio: First Black Milwaukee County executive, elected in 2020. Former state representative.

positions.topic: Jobs and Wages That Build Futures
positions.statement: Crowley will grow an economy rooted in local ownership and union labor.
As Governor, Crowley will modernize and expand apprenticeship programs—connecting young people and career-changers to opportunities in the trades, health care, green energy, and beyond.

Crowley will make Wisconsin the best place in the Midwest to start and grow a small business — whether you're opening a bakery in Beloit, running a barbershop in Wausau, or launching a biotech startup in Eau Claire.

candidates.name: Francesca Hong
candidates.bio: Third-term Assembly representative from Madison and Wisconsin's first Asian American legislator. Chef and former restaurant owner.

positions.statement: No child should go hungry. We have the means to help.
Nobody should have to decide between paying the bills and taking care of themselves or a sick family member.
The only health finance model that can address the core issues of our healthcare system is federal Medicare for All.

candidates.name: Missy Hughes
candidates.occupation: Former Wisconsin Economic Development Corp. CEO
candidates.bio: Led the Wisconsin Economic Development Corp. from 2019 to 2025.

positions.topic: As Governor, Missy Will Create a Main Street Economy
positions.statement: Ensure Wisconsinites have higher wages and housing they can afford

candidates.status_note: Suspended her campaign on June 22, 2026. Her name remains on the primary ballot.

candidates.name: Sara Rodriguez
candidates.bio: Elected lieutenant governor in 2022. Nurse and former state representative.

positions.statement: As governor, Sara Rodriguez will lower prices and guarantee affordable, reliable health care for every person in Wisconsin.
As governor, Sara Rodriguez will lower property taxes for Wisconsin residents.

positions.topic: Make Child Care Affordable for Families
positions.statement: As governor, Sara Rodriguez will make child care affordable and accessible for Wisconsin's working families.

candidates.name: Kelda Roys
candidates.bio: State senator serving on the Legislature's Joint Finance Committee. Former Assembly representative, born in central Wisconsin.

positions.topic: STANDING UP TO TRUMP AND TIFFANY
positions.statement: President Trump and Congressman Tiffany's policies are making us all less safe, less free, less prosperous, and less healthy.
Wages are not keeping up with the rising costs of groceries, rent and housing expenses, healthcare, prescription drugs, utilities, transportation, or childcare.
A high quality public education is the birthright of every Wisconsin child — from early childhood to higher ed.
Originally I wanted to go all the way and say after 30 years you shouldn't have to pay for your home anymore.
I am a big believer that elections should be secure, and nobody should leave election day feeling unsure if their vote was counted.
I am bringing my own idea of a new plan to the office and public. It is not government run, but government adjacent.

candidates.name: Tom Tiffany
candidates.photo_credit: U.S. House Office of the Clerk
candidates.bio: Represents northern Wisconsin's 7th Congressional District. Former state senator and state representative.

positions.statement: Alongside President Trump, Tom delivered the largest tax cut in history for working families, seniors, and small businesses. He ended taxes on tips, overtime, and Social Security.
Tom will restore order and create opportunity for our great state. That means building again. Manufacturing again.
When Tom talks to parents across Wisconsin—from Milwaukee to Hayward, Green Bay to Eau Claire—they all say the same thing: they want schools that actually prepare their kids for success.

races.office_note: First in line to serve as governor if that office becomes vacant; other duties are largely assigned by the governor. Nominated separately in the primary, then elected on a joint ticket with the governor's nominee in November. Four-year term.
races.context: Lt. Gov. Sara Rodriguez is running for governor rather than seeking re-election. Primary winners will join their party's gubernatorial nominee on the November ticket.

coverage.headline: Wisconsin Secretary of State Sarah Godlewski enters the 2026 race for lieutenant governor
Small business owner Will Martin launches campaign for lieutenant governor

candidates.name: Sarah Godlewski
candidates.bio: Appointed secretary of state in 2023. Former state treasurer.

positions.statement: For far too long, energy production and environmental stewardship have been treated as competing goals. I reject that premise.

positions.topic: Back the Blue & Red: A Plan for a Safer Wisconsin
positions.statement: Public safety is the first responsibility of state and local government. Every day, Wisconsin's law enforcement officers, firefighters, EMS workers, and 911 dispatchers protect the people of our state, yet they often do not receive the support and appreciation they deserve from leadership.

candidates.name: David Varnam
candidates.bio: Former mayor of Lancaster, elected in 2016. Former congressional aide and federal policy analyst.

positions.statement: I will work to lower taxes to keep our retirees, young people, and businesses in Wisconsin. As mayor, I vetoed a 40% increase in the stormwater fee and defeated a proposed wheel tax.
As a Father of two daughters, I will fight to keep men out of women's sports and protect their locker rooms.

Wisconsin needs a new Republican Administration in Madison that will work with President Trump. As Lieutenant Governor, I will ensure that our state and local law enforcement work with federal immigration agencies to deport criminal illegal aliens.

races.office_note: The state's chief lawyer: leads the Wisconsin Department of Justice, represents the state in court, issues formal opinions on state law, and oversees the state crime laboratories. Four-year term.
races.context: Attorney General Josh Kaul is seeking a third term and is unopposed in the Democratic primary. Eric Toney is unopposed in the Republican primary.

coverage.headline: Kaul calls Toney a 'yes man' for Trump as he prepares for rematch
Republican Eric Toney is running again for Wisconsin attorney general
Kaul to run for a third term as Wisconsin Attorney General in 2026 election
Toney enters race for Wisconsin attorney general, touting 'public safety over politics'

positions.statement: Law and order are on the line. Violent criminals walk free while families live in fear.
Fentanyl and meth are flooding into our communities, killing our kids, and fueling crime.
When crime labs fail, justice fails. I'll clean up the mess—modernize our labs, end the backlog, and hold leadership accountable.

races.office_note: Keeper of the state's official acts and the Great Seal. Unlike in many states, Wisconsin's secretary of state has no role in running elections — the Wisconsin Elections Commission administers those. Four-year term.
races.context: Secretary of State Sarah Godlewski is not seeking re-election; she is running for lieutenant governor instead.

coverage.headline: Milwaukee Alderperson Running For Secretary of State
Ald. JoCasta Zamarripa announces campaign for Wisconsin Secretary of State

positions.statement: Across Wisconsin, families are feeling squeezed. Prices are up, but wages and support haven't kept pace.
JoCasta has led the way on housing solutions in Milwaukee and understands how deeply the affordability crisis is affecting families across the state.
The rising cost and limited availability of child care is holding back families and our economy.
After half a century of failed Democratic leadership, the Secretary of State's office is a shadow of its former self.
Brayden will work to form an easily accessible digital database of all the records this office holds and overlooks.
Brayden believes timeliness is essential for a government to properly serve its citizens.
The Secretary of State is elected, so voters can directly hold them accountable for how our elections are run.
36 states put elections under the Secretary of State. Along with overseeing public records and land funds, the office must stand for transparency and accountability.
Push the Legislature to restore elections to the Secretary of State from the corrupt WEC—by referendum if needed. Wisconsin's election integrity cannot be compromised.

races.office_note: A constitutional office with few remaining statutory duties. The treasurer serves on the Board of Commissioners of Public Lands, which manages state trust lands and funds that support public school libraries. Four-year term.
races.context: State Treasurer John S. Leiber is unopposed in the Republican primary. Two Democrats, Dylan Helmenstine and Yee Leng Xiong, are competing for their party's nomination.

coverage.headline: Two Democrats seek to challenge Treasurer John Leiber, Wisconsin's only GOP state office holder
Wausau area man launches run for state treasurer
Dane County native announced bid for Wisconsin State Treasurer

positions.topic: 1. Clarity on the Systems That Impact us Today
positions.statement: Through careful planning and citizen involvement, Wisconsinites can come together to move our economy forward. But right now, our broken funding rules, overlapping levels of government, and outdated laws are getting in the way of that.
Budgets are statements of our values. That is true for our family, business, and government budgets alike.

positions.topic: 3. An Engine to Move Our State Forward
positions.statement: It is time to revive the State Treasurer's role in pulling people together to find new ways forward for Wisconsin communities. Our broken systems of governance and funding are holding us back.

positions.topic: Protect & Grow Wisconsin's School Trust Fund
positions.statement: Every dollar invested wisely means more resources for our kids.

positions.topic: Support Local Communities with Smart Infrastructure Investment
positions.statement: Helping communities build what they need—without overburdening taxpayers.
A Treasurer who treats your money with respect.
